text: Ebia anka nneɛma a wɔrese no sɛ ɔrentumi nyɛ no nyinaa no yɛ deɛ ɔbɛtumi ayɛ ama no aboro so mpo, nanso ɛsiane sɛ akɔba no sei na n'adwene agye atom nti no, daa ɔhunu ne ho sɛ biribiara nni hɔ a ɔbɛtumi ayɛ.
ref: Perhaps all the things he is being told he cannot do would have been even more than he could have done for him, but because it has come to him this way and his mind has accepted it, he always finds himself with nothing he can do.
translated: If it were possible for all the good things they expected of him to actually happen, he would have done even more, but because of the severity of his illness and his weakened mind, they realized he couldn't do anything at all.

text: Deɛ nti a ɛte saa ne sɛ, ahokyerɛ a ɛwɔ amansini binom te sɛ Suaman mu no yɛ (6.0%), Wassa Amenfi Atɔeɛ deɛ yɛ (6.9%), ɛnna Aowin nso kura ɛmu (8.9%) pɛ.
ref: the reason is that, the poverty rate in some district like the Suaman district is just 6.0%, Wassa Amenfi West has 6.9%, and Aowin also has 8.9%.
translated: This is because the representation in the constituencies is like that of Suaman (6.0%), Wassa Amenfi East (6.9%), and Aowin also accounts for 8.9% respectively.

text: Yεatwa nnua akεseε nyinaa ama akwaeε adane mfofoo ne asase pεsεε
ref: Because of deforestation, our forest has become desert and wilderness.
translated: We have released all the water for the foolish/silly person and the earth to enjoy.

text: Mmom, ɛhyɛ aseɛ firi Otumfoɔ Ɔsɛe Tutu I so ɛfiri sɛ, ne pɛn so na Ɔkɔmfo Anɔkye yii Sika dwa Kofi maa Asanteman.
ref: It started from Otumfo Osei Tutu 1 , because it was during his era that Okomfo Anokye Commanded the Golden stool
translated: It is said that it originated from Otumfoɔ Osei Tutu I, because Okɔmfo Anɔkye gave the Golden Stool, Kofi, to Asanteman.

text: Onipa no brԑ wᴐ adidie ne ᴐnom ho.
ref: The person is tired of eating and drinking.
translated: People's wealth is in their name.

text: Bio nso, na mmaayewa ne mmeranteɛ nyinaa hwɛ wɔn ho so yie na ama wɔatumi akwati animguaseɛ a ɛte saa ɛno nti, tete no, na mperewanyinsɛn nnɔɔso sɛ ɛnnɛ.
ref: Furthermore, due to the responsible behavior and self-care practices among the young ladies and gentlemen in the towns or villages, the incidence of teenage pregnancy was significantly lower compared to the present times.
translated: It is good, and may all the young and old take good care of themselves and be able to overcome this difficult situation, that is why I said it, and I don't think it will be easy.

text: Akanfoɔ amammerɛ mu no, afadeɛ ne ahosiesie yɛ biribi a wɔntoto no ase koraa. wohwɛ mu a, wɔn ahosiesie ka nneɛma ahodoɔ a ɛma wɔda nso firi kasakuo afoforɔ ho no ho.
ref: In Akan culture, festivals and dress are not underestimated. you see, their dress is among the various factors that also distinguish them from other dialects.
translated: In Akan literature, decay and ruin are often something deeply rooted. Look closely, their ruin speaks of the many things that also prevent them from benefiting from the arrival of new ideas.

text: Bɔ mmɔden na taa tenetene w'apom.
ref: Try to exercise regularly.
translated: Be patient and take things slowly.

text: Ɛsiane sɛ na Bafo Pem suro sɛ Abonofoɔ yi bɛtumi ayi no ama nti, ɔmaa wɔnomm abosom; ɛne sɛ, wɔtwaa wɔn mogya guu nsuom maa obiara nomm bi.
ref: since Bafo Pem was afraid that the of Bono will be taken off the throne, he gave them gods, that is , they spilled their blood into water to be taken by all.
translated: It is said that because Bafo Pem wanted the foreigners to not be able to harm him, he offered a sacrifice to a god; and they poured their blood into the river so that no one would take any.

text: Wɔn akraboa ne anene (kwaakwaadabi), ne asona wɔ.
ref: Their totem is crow and wild boar.
translated: They have a strong and beautiful voice (like a parrot), and they also have a drum.

text: Adeɛ baako pa ara nti a nnipa yɛ mmɔntohɔ ne osuro anaasɛ ehu.
ref: One of the main reasons people are procrastinated is fear or dread.
translated: One good head is better than many hands or strength.
